steps.title: Identify the subject structure
steps.content: The subject is "Amplifying the voices and experiences of Black soldiers," a gerund phrase (the -ing form acting as a noun). Gerund phrases are singular.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Rule: a gerund phrase ("Amplifying the voices and experiences of Black soldiers") acts as a singular noun and requires a singular verb. The passage describes Chester's past work during the Civil War, so past tense is also required.

A — "were" Plural past. "Amplifying...were" treats the gerund phrase as plural. Fails.

B — "have been" Plural present perfect. Plural and wrong tense for a historical statement about Chester. Fails.

C — "are" Plural present. Plural and shifts to present tense, contradicting the past-tense context of the passage. Fails.

D — "was" Singular past. Matches the singular gerund subject and the historical past-tense context of Chester's Civil War work. Correct.

D is correct.
